articles.title: Give Your AI Eyes: Introducing Chrome DevTools MCP
articles.description: The Chrome team has released an MCP server for Chrome DevTools, enabling agents like Claude Code or OpenAI Codex to use the DevTools to debug and analyze the performance and behavior of your webapps (or even just to automate the use of Chrome generally). Addy does a great job of explaining the potential here.
articles.author: Addy Osmani

articles.title: GitHub's Plan for a More Secure npm Supply Chain
articles.description: In direct response to the recent npm ecosystem supply chain attacks of recent weeks, GitHub’s senior director of security research has outlined steps GitHub is taking, including blocking the upload of packages featuring the patterns of the recent malware, hardening package publication, and promoting the use of trusted publishing.
articles.author: Xavier René-Corail (GitHub)

articles.title: From Steam to Floppy: Porting Modern TypeScript to Run on DOS
articles.description: The creator of a DOS-inspired programming game (available on Steam) wanted to try and get the game running on real DOS. Thanks to jSH, a JavaScript engine for DOS, it was kinda do-able.
articles.author: Jimbly / Dashing Strike

articles.title: NPM Security Best Practices
articles.description: An extensive list of best practices, techniques, and ideas to consider for making your use of the npm packaging ecosystem and its tooling more secure.
articles.author: Boda

articles.title: JSON is Not JSON Across Languages
articles.description: If you use JSON to communicate between systems built in different languages, beware. Different libraries with varying opinions can cause “some of the most soul-crushing debugging experiences in software development.”
articles.author: Dochia CLI

articles.title: 🤖 GitHub Copilot CLI Now in Public Preview
articles.description: Not content to let Claude Code and OpenAI Codex dominate the CLI-based dev agent scene, GitHub has released a CLI-based version of Copilot, built using Node.
articles.author: GitHub

articles.title: TanStack Start v1 Release Candidate
articles.description: TanStack’s attempt at a full-stack TanStack Router-powered framework has reached a v1.0 release candidate that’s expected to be largely the same as its eventual 1.0 release. “It’s the next chapter in building type-safe, high-performance React apps without the heavy abstractions.”
articles.author: Tanner Linsley

articles.title: Cap'n Web: A New RPC System for Browsers and Web Servers
articles.description: A ‘spiritual sibling’ to Cap’n Proto, an RPC protocol created by one of the same authors. However, Cap’n Web’s underlying serialization is human-readable, focused on integrating well with JS runtimes, and works over HTTP, WebSocket, and postMessage() out-of-the-box.
articles.author: Varda and Faulkner (Cloudflare)

articles.title: eslint-plugin-react-you-might-not-need-an-effect
articles.description: What a project name! It’s an ESLint plugin to help React developers catch unnecessary useEffects which are quite commonly overused.
articles.author: Nick van Dyke

articles.description: Netscape Navigator 2.0 Beta was released 30 years ago – it was notable as the first browser to include JavaScript support.
The agenda of this week's TC39 meeting where several proposals were advanced, including Import Bytes, Iterator Chunking, and Array.prototype.pushAll.
The Chrome team expects Chrome/Chromium's support for the Temporal API to land in Chromium 144 (the current stable version is 140).
InfoQ shares an update on the features of Preact 11 beta, the recently released update to the popular lightweight React alternative.
The IEEE has released its list of the top programming languages of 2025 with JavaScript and TypeScript coming in at #6 and #7 respectively.
pnpm 10.17 – The new minimumReleaseAgeExclude now supports patterns to exclude groups of packages from minimum release age checks.
Astro 5.14 - Boasts "a bumper harvest of features and DX improvements to warm the cockles of your heart."
Node.js v24.9.0 (Current) and v22.20.0 (LTS)
Nuxt UI 4.0, PostgreSQL 18.0, ESLint v9.36.0

articles.title: How Much Do You Really Know About Media Queries?
articles.description: Just as Daniel discovered, there’s probably more to media queries than you might have assumed. There’s plenty of descriptors available to us, including aspect-ratio, monochrome, vertical-viewport-segments, and more. “many of them definitely deserve more attention”. He digs into a bunch right here.
articles.author: Daniel Schwarz

articles.title: Touring New CSS Features in Safari 26
articles.description: Last week we shared news of Safari 26’s release, and now Juan has dug into what’s new, sharing the rundown of what’s now supported and what these additions open up.
articles.author: Juan Diego Rodríguez

articles.title: CSS Wish: Inner Breakpoints
articles.description: Dr. Axel explains why he wants to see CSS support ‘inner breakpoints’ – breakpoints not for viewports or containers but for HTML elements inside viewports or containers.
articles.author: Dr. Axel Rauschmayer

articles.title: In The Economy of User Effort, Be a Bargain, Not a Scam
articles.description: “Treat user effort as a currency. To create a product users love, design the tradeoff curve of use case complexity to user effort with the same care you design your pricing scheme”.
articles.author: Lea Verou

articles.title: Styling Siblings with CSS Has Never Been Easier
articles.description: Some experimentation with sibling-count and sibling-index, demos and visuals included.
articles.author: Brecht De Ruyte

articles.title: A Complete Guide to HTTP Caching
articles.description: Covers everything from the high-level questions such as why you need to cache, and what to cache, down to things like validation, keys, browser behaviors, CDNs, debugging, and more.
articles.author: Jono Alderson

articles.title: WCAG’s Longevity
articles.description: A fair critique of how some aspects of the accessibility guidelines were created “under the premise of a 2005-era web that do not hold true for 2025”, followed by a specific look at an audio description section of the standard.
articles.author: Eric Eggert

articles.title: The Best CSS Unit Might Be a Combination
articles.description: Now that we have comparison functions, we don’t have to choose between px and rem for spacing.
articles.author: Miriam Suzanne

articles.title: The Coyier CSS Starter
articles.description: A fairly opinionated CSS starter/reset by Chris Coyier — it follows a set of personal principals to guide what is in there and what isn’t, as explained here.
articles.author: Chris Coyier

articles.title: ▶  The corner-shape CSS Property is Amazing
articles.description: Here’s a brief look at this new property and what creative options it unlocks.
articles.author: Zoran Jambor

articles.title: Polypane 26: Accurate Device Emulation with Safe Area and Small Viewport Units
articles.description: Version 26 of this developer-focused browser dropped just a few days ago, and this release makes Polypane the first browser to let you emulate safe-area-inset values and small viewport heights. A win for accurately testing emulated devices.
articles.author: Kilian Valkhof

articles.title: React Cookie Manager: A Powerful, Customizable React Component for Cookie Consent
articles.description: A well-documented solution that will automatically block tracking if necessary and can easily be customized. Try the live demo here — which is also an interactive page demonstrating its features.
articles.author: hypership

articles.title: HTML3D: A Lightweight JavaScript Library for Creating Interactive 3D Scenes using CSS 3D Transforms
articles.description: The demo is neat. It includes an inception-like ‘game within a game’ with the ability to edit the game’s code right inside the game itself.
articles.author: Tim Samshuijzen

articles.title: eslint-plugin-depend: An ESLint Plugin to Help Suggest Alternatives to Various Dependencies
articles.description: For example, it will detect dependency tree bloat and polyfills that can now be removed due to redundancy.
articles.author: ES Community

articles.title: Svelte Animations: Animated UI Components Built with Svelte
articles.description: Inspired by similar libraries for React (Magic UI, Acernity, Luxe, etc). Many of the components have Svelte 5 support, and you can browse them here.
articles.author: Sikandar Bhide

articles.title: 'Messenger'
articles.description: This is a beautifully animated example of just what’s now possible in the browser. A charming little Web-GL interactive experience. Take a moment to explore.
articles.author: Abeto
